name: Regime Tradeoff Matrix
summary: Compare regimes by legitimacy, accountability, freedom, speed, coercion risk, and correction mechanisms.
whenToUseIt: Use this when comparing political systems, reading history, or trying to understand why different regimes attract support despite obvious risks.
steps: Choose the regimes you want to compare.
Score each on legitimacy source, freedom, accountability, speed, coercion risk, and correction mechanisms.
Write one genuine upside for each model.
Write one predictable failure mode for each model.
Ask what daily life would feel like for an ordinary citizen in each case.
example: Liberal democracy scores better on lawful opposition and correction, while authoritarian systems often score higher on short-term speed but lower on accountability and citizen freedom.
